name: Debopama Basu
bio: I'm Debopama, an M.Tech student in System-on-Chip Design at IIT Palakkad, currently researching hardware security and post-quantum cryptography at IIT Kharagpur's SEAL Lab under Prof. Debdeep Mukhopadhyay. Before academia, I spent two years as a software engineer at Cognizant, working on application security for a fintech firm. I'm drawn to the intersection of hardware and security — understanding how systems can be broken so they can be built better. When I'm not staring at power traces or Verilog code, I'm probably singing.

experience.description: Working under Prof. Debdeep Mukhopadhyay on hardware security and post-quantum cryptography. Performing side-channel attacks on cryptographic algorithms, building a side-channel resilient hardware security module in an IIT KGP incubated startup, and conducting deep learning-based side-channel analysis with hyperparameter exploration.

Worked on vulnerability remediation for a fintech payments firm as part of the Application Security team. Remediated OWASP Top 10 vulnerabilities across Java Spring Boot microservices using Fortify, managed CI/CD pipelines via Jenkins, and led migration of microservices from Java 8 to Java 11. Achieved Microsoft Azure Fundamentals (AZ-900) certification.
Built frontend interfaces using HTML5, CSS3, JavaScript and Angular. Developed backend logic with Java Spring Boot, wrote JUnit tests, and integrated MySQL databases using JDBC.

projects.title: 32-bit RISC-V Processor Simulator
projects.description: Designed a 32-bit single-cycle RISC-V processor simulator in Python, then extended it to a 5-stage pipelined processor with hazard detection, forwarding unit, branch predictor, and instruction/data caching. Validated functionality using benchmark programs.

projects.title: SoC Hardware Accelerators
projects.description: Designed hardware accelerators for 512-point FFT, FIR filtering, image convolution, and DC component removal using Vivado HLS. Implemented on FPGA via HLx workflow and validated outputs against MATLAB results.

projects.title: 1-D & 2-D DCT via Systolic Array
projects.description: Implemented Discrete Cosine Transform using systolic array architecture, exploring efficient hardware design patterns for signal processing applications.

projects.title: Verilog VLSI Modules
projects.description: Developed Verilog modules for traffic light controller (FSM), N-bit Fibonacci generator, CORDIC (iterative & pipelined), GCD, FIR filter, and synchronous counters. Wrote testbenches and performed timing analysis for power and area optimization.

projects.title: Multi-threaded Performance Optimization
projects.description: Analyzed and optimized a tiled Matrix Multiplication application using Intel VTune. Progressively improved performance using pthreads, OpenMP, SIMD vectorization, and hierarchical tiling.
